matches.lb: Quoting Commentary: Ibn Ezra on Genesis 38:1:1
matches.x: AND IT CAME TO PASS AT THAT TIME. This does not refer to the time that Joseph was sold.1As the sequence of chapters would tend to indicate. It refers to the events that happened prior to that time. From thence they journeyed unto Gudgod…At that time the Lord separated the tribe of Levi, to bear the ark of the covenant of the Lord, to stand before the Lord to minister unto Him, and to bless in His…

47. Al Khazari: Certainly, and rightly so. I think I read in your books as follows: 'What doth the Lord thy God require of thee, but to fear the Lord thy God' (Deuteronom 10:12) and 'What doth the Lord require of thee' (Micah 6:8), and many similar passages?

47. Al Khazari: Certainly, and rightly so. I think I read in your books as follows: 'What doth the Lord thy God require of thee, but to fear the Lord thy God' (Deuteronom 10:12) and 'What doth the Lord require of thee' (Micah 6:8), and many similar passages?

47. Al Khazari: Certainly, and rightly so. I think I read in your books as follows: 'What doth the Lord thy God require of thee, but to fear the Lord thy God' (Deuteronom 10:12) and 'What doth the Lord require of thee' (Micah 6:8), and many similar passages?

47. Al Khazari: Certainly, and rightly so. I think I read in your books as follows: 'What doth the Lord thy God require of thee, but to fear the Lord thy God' (Deuteronom 10:12) and 'What doth the Lord require of thee' (Micah 6:8), and many similar passages?

Remember, that ours is the true and authentic Divine religion, revealed to us through Moses, the master of the former as well as the later prophets, by means of which God has distinguished us from the rest of mankind, as Scripture says, "Only the Lord had a delight in thy fathers to love them and He chose their seed after them, even you above all peoples" (Deuteronomy 10:15). This did not happen…

2 Circumcise the foreskin of your flesh and appear (like us), and be sealed like us in the circumcision οἵ... and we will be to y(ou) b(rethren).
An uncircumcised male, who will not circumcise his foreskin, that soul [man] shall be cut off from its people; he has broken [changed] My covenant.
2 Circumcise the foreskin of your flesh and appear (like us), and be sealed like us in the circumcision οἵ... and we will be to y(ou) b(rethren).
An uncircumcised male, who will not circumcise his foreskin, that soul [man] shall be cut off from its people; he has broken [changed] My covenant.

CHAPTER X. Moses’ rehearsal continued—An exhortation unto obedience. T that time: the Lord said unto me, Hew thee two other tables of stone like unto the first, and come up unto. me upon the mount, and make thee an ark of wood.

Without any respect of persons] "For the Lord your God is God of gods, and Lord of lords, a great God, a mighty and a terrible, which regardeth not persons, nor taketh reward" (Deuteronomy 10:17). See also Alma 1:30; Romans 2:11; James 2:1-9. (Joseph Fielding Mc Conkie and Robert L. Millet, Doctrinal Commentary on the Book of Mormon, 4 vols., 3:, p.121)

It is related that the convert Onkelos9Onkelos the proselyte translated the Bible into Aramaic. asked one of the elders: How can the proselyte be so precious to the Holy One, blessed be He if, in return for his devotion, it is written merely: And He loveth a stranger in giving him food and raiment (Deut. 10:18)? He replied: Even our patriarch Jacob sought no more than that from the Holy One,…

matches.lb: Second Temple: On the Migration of Abraham 24:1
matches.x: [132] Using still loftier language to express the irrepressible craving for moral excellence, he calls on them to cleave to Him. His words are: “Thou shalt fear the Lord thy God, and Him shalt thou serve, and to Him shalt thou cleave” (Deut. 10:20). What then is the cementing substance? Do you ask, what? Piety, surely, and faith: for these virtues adjust and unite the intent of the heart to the…
